scenarios.title: Goal-Line Stand
scenarios.description: Carrier is within ~50 HU of goal; defender is charging. Defender must be able to deny score if the hit occurs before the goal condition resolves.

expected_outcomes.description: Carrier is tackled or knocked down before goal condition resolves; no score registered.
After the stop, ball is loose or transferred — not frozen in place.

must_not_outcomes.description: Carrier scores even though the hit clearly preceded the goal condition.

scenarios.notes: Hit-before-goal timing must be deterministic, not frame-dependent.

scenarios.title: Panic Short Pass
scenarios.description: Carrier is swarmed by two defenders. Early or low-power throw must create a nearby contested loose ball — not a safe clean pass.

expected_outcomes.description: Ball lands nearby as a loose ball with multiple players able to contest it.

must_not_outcomes.description: Ball reaches a teammate cleanly with no contest — panic throw should not produce a perfect result.
Carrier retains ball despite throw input — voluntary release must work.

scenarios.notes: Throw power/arc must degrade meaningfully under pressure. The loose ball landing zone should be close enough for both teams to realistically contest.

scenarios.title: Long Throw Recovery
scenarios.description: Carrier throws a high arc downfield into open space. Ball must be recoverable and create a new contest — not vanish, clip geometry, or become dead.

expected_outcomes.description: Ball lands in bounds, remains live, and can be picked up by any player.
Players converge on landing zone — a new spatial conflict forms.

must_not_outcomes.description: Ball passes through geometry and becomes unrecoverable.
Ball never settles and continues rolling past any recoverable position.
Ball is too fast or bounces erratically — no player can practically contest it.

scenarios.notes: Arc trajectory must respect gravity in a way players can read and predict. Out-of-bounds resets must not grant possession.

scenarios.title: Jump-Flung Fumble
scenarios.description: Carrier jumps and is hit or loses control during vertical motion. Verticality must produce readable chaos with a contestable ball — not a dead straight-down drop.

expected_outcomes.description: Ball travels in a direction related to the collision — readable momentum transfer.
Ball comes to rest or bounces in a location where players can realistically contest it.

must_not_outcomes.description: Ball falls directly below the carrier with no spatial influence from the hit.

scenarios.notes: Jump + hit interaction is a primary EFT skill expression. The output direction should be predictable enough for experienced players to anticipate.

scenarios.title: Swarm Collapse
scenarios.description: Loose ball with four or more players converging. Collisions, knockdowns, and possible ball pop-out must produce a new local crisis — not a ghost-through.

expected_outcomes.description: Players collide, causing knockdowns, direction changes, or ball pop-out — not just path-through.
After the swarm, a new unstable situation exists — ball in contest, knockdowns recovering, contested pickup imminent.

must_not_outcomes.description: Players pass through each other with no physics or gameplay consequence.

scenarios.notes: This is the core chaos test. Bloodbowl centre-field is the canonical environment for this scenario.

scenarios.title: Midfield Collection
scenarios.description: Ball lobbed across the middle; defender cuts the lane. Defender must be able to collect by running through the ball path — no special catch key or animation confirmation required.

expected_outcomes.description: Defender automatically picks up the ball by moving through its path.

must_not_outcomes.description: Collection requires a button press or a scripted catch animation — violates automatic pickup.

scenarios.notes: Automatic pickup is non-negotiable (P-950). The key test is that no additional input beyond movement is required.

scenarios.title: Escort Clearing
scenarios.description: Carrier follows a teammate. Defender approaches the carrier. Teammate must be able to tackle/block the defender so the carrier can continue.

expected_outcomes.description: Teammate collides with defender, causing knockdown or direction change that protects the carrier.
Carrier continues forward after the block without being immediately stopped.

must_not_outcomes.description: Defender passes through or ignores teammate and tackles carrier as if escort did not exist.

scenarios.notes: Tests that player bodies matter in open play — not just carrier/ball interactions.

scenarios.title: Intentional Hazard Reset
scenarios.description: Carrier is pinned near own goal. Team uses an intentional hazard or void dump to force a neutral reset rather than concede possession. Reset must be fast and fair — not a long downtime or a gift.

expected_outcomes.description: Ball resets to a neutral location with no team retaining possession advantage.
Reset completes quickly — no extended dead-ball period.

must_not_outcomes.description: The dumping team re-obtains the ball immediately after reset — hazard reset becomes a free re-possession.
Reset causes >5 seconds of dead play.

scenarios.notes: Map-dependent: only valid on maps with accessible hazards/voids. Slam Dunk and Bloodbowl both qualify.

scenarios.title: Head-On Speed Duel
scenarios.description: Two players charge head-on. Player A is moving at a clearly higher speed than Player B. Higher-speed player should win the collision or close-speed cases resolve readably — never randomly.

expected_outcomes.description: Player A (higher speed) wins the collision — Player B is knocked down or deflected.

must_not_outcomes.description: Winner is determined by RNG rather than speed/skill inputs.

scenarios.notes: P-060 is explicit: skill delta must be legible. Close-speed outcomes can be defined (e.g. mutual knockdown) but must not be random. Speed includes powerup-modified speeds.

scenarios.title: Last-Second Touchdown Stop
scenarios.description: Carrier is airborne or running directly into goal. Defender lands a hit just before the scoring condition triggers. Score must be deniable if hit precedes goal.

expected_outcomes.description: No goal registered; ball becomes loose or possession transfers.

must_not_outcomes.description: Carrier within goal proximity becomes invulnerable — hit has no effect on scoring.

scenarios.notes: Related to S-001 but specifically tests the airborne + last-frame case. Timing resolution must be deterministic.

scenarios.title: Loose Ball Bounce
scenarios.description: Ball bounces after a fumble or throw. Bounce trajectory must be readable and the landing zone contestable.

expected_outcomes.description: Ball reflects off surface in a direction consistent with incidence angle — players can anticipate it.
Ball comes to a rest or second bounce in a location where nearby players can realistically compete for it.

must_not_outcomes.description: Ball loses all momentum instantly on contact — no bounce or roll.
Ball bounces in an unreadable direction with no spatial logic.

scenarios.notes: Wall bounces on Slam Dunk are particularly important — platforms and side walls create rebound routes that skilled players exploit.

scenarios.title: Respawn Rejoin
scenarios.description: Player dies while ball is actively contested. Respawn path must allow the player to return to an active or immediately subsequent contest within ~10 seconds.

expected_outcomes.description: Respawned player can reach the active contest or the next one within ~10 seconds.

must_not_outcomes.description: Respawn location or timing locks the player out of play for more than 10 seconds.

scenarios.notes: Spawn placement is map-critical. All EFT maps must be validated against this — spawn clusters that are too far from the contest center will fail.

scenarios.title: Choke Corridor Fight
scenarios.description: Ball is in a narrow corridor or choke point. Body-blocking and clearing maneuvers must matter — defending a choke must be viable, not random.

expected_outcomes.description: Defender can use their body to block or slow the ball carrier's path through the choke.
A team can clear defenders from the choke by outrunning or hitting them, allowing a carrier through.

must_not_outcomes.description: Player bodies have no effect in the choke — carrier passes through freely.
Outcome is coin-flip with no relation to positioning or skill.

scenarios.notes: Slam Dunk's side corridors and Bloodbowl's channel edges are the canonical environments.

scenarios.title: Score Counter-Attack
scenarios.description: Team A scores. Ball resets. Team B can immediately rush the reset point to create a new contest — no long dead-play interruption.

expected_outcomes.description: New contest is live within a few seconds of the reset. Team B can realistically rush and challenge.

must_not_outcomes.description: Goal triggers a ceremony or pause that removes the ability to immediately counter.

scenarios.notes: The counter-rush after a goal is a core EFT tension pattern. The reset delay must be short enough that Team B can contest the reset ball if they commit immediately.

scenarios.title: Tackle Chain
scenarios.description: Player A tackles B; ball pops to C; Player D immediately tackles C. Rapid chained possession/knockdown events must be possible — cooldowns must not block the second valid tackle.

expected_outcomes.description: A tackles B; ball becomes loose and goes to C.
D can tackle C immediately — no cooldown prevents the second valid tackle.
After both tackles, ball is in a new loose or newly possessed state.

must_not_outcomes.description: A global or per-player tackle cooldown prevents D from immediately tackling C after A tackles B.

scenarios.notes: The chain should be possible within a single second. This tests that cooldown systems are per-player (not global) and short enough to preserve EFT's pace.

scenarios.title: Goal-Line Intercept
scenarios.description: Loose ball rolling or flying toward the goal. A defending player must be able to cut it off and intercept it — ball must not become non-interactable near scoring.

expected_outcomes.description: Defender collects the ball by moving into its path — automatic pickup applies.

must_not_outcomes.description: Ball ignores the defender near the goal zone — either phased through or treated as already scored.

scenarios.notes: Critical for Slam Dunk where hoops are elevated — ball arc toward hoop must be interceptable by a jumping defender.

scenarios.title: Mid-Air Catch / Pickup
scenarios.description: Ball thrown high; a player jumps into its path. Player must be able to collect through contact — automatic pickup, not a scripted catch. Player carries momentum state from the jump.

expected_outcomes.description: Player automatically picks up ball while airborne by moving through its path.
Player's airborne momentum state is preserved after collection — no snap-to-ground.

must_not_outcomes.description: Pickup only possible when grounded or via a catch animation — mid-air body contact fails.

scenarios.notes: The Slam Dunk slam-dunk route depends on this — jumping toward the hoop while collecting a thrown ball is the key interaction.

scenarios.title: Powerup Route
scenarios.description: Carrier chooses a longer route through a speedball or powerup. The powerup must change tempo enough to be worth the detour — but the route must remain contestable by defenders anticipating it.

expected_outcomes.description: Powerup effect is significant enough that the longer route was worth taking — speed, score multiplier, or other effect is impactful.
Defender who anticipates the powerup route can still challenge — powerup does not guarantee success.

must_not_outcomes.description: Powerup effect is too small to justify the detour — route choice is irrelevant.
Powerup makes the carrier effectively unstoppable — defenders have no realistic counter.

scenarios.notes: Speedball is the canonical powerup. Scoreball and iceball have different risk profiles — test all variants where available.

scenarios.title: Carrier Juke
scenarios.description: Defender charges straight at carrier. Carrier cuts sideways or uses geometry to cause the defender to overcommit. Defender must lose relevance for a short window — no magnetic tracking.

expected_outcomes.description: Defender's charge carries them past or away from the carrier, opening a window.
Carrier has a brief window of reduced threat — not immunity, just a gap.

must_not_outcomes.description: Defender auto-corrects direction perfectly and the juke produces no meaningful separation.

scenarios.notes: Jukes are map-geometry-dependent. Slam Dunk platforms and Bloodbowl open field produce different juke windows.

scenarios.title: Bot Positioning
scenarios.description: Ball is loose to one side of the field. A bot must move toward the predicted future contest or pickup zone — not chase the current ball position if it has already moved.

expected_outcomes.description: Bot anticipates where the ball will be and moves there, not where the ball was.

must_not_outcomes.description: Bot moves to the ball's stale last-known position and arrives after the contest has moved.

scenarios.notes: Bot AI must use predictive positioning (C-005 domain). This scenario validates the AI target selection, not physics.

scenarios.title: Slam Dunk Hoop Decision
scenarios.description: Slam Dunk scoring complex has throw and movement-assisted slam-dunk routes. The map analyzer and game implementation must distinguish these scoring methods and preserve the route pressure each creates.

expected_outcomes.description: Map analyzer correctly identifies the throw-to-hoop route as a distinct scoring method.
Map analyzer correctly identifies the movement-assisted dunk route as a distinct scoring method.
Implementation treats the two routes as requiring different defensive responses — not collapsed into one generic goal check.

must_not_outcomes.description: Both scoring routes reduced to a single trigger_goal box with no route distinction.

scenarios.notes: This is a map-intelligence scenario as much as a gameplay scenario. The map analyzer must produce distinct scoring complex entries for throw vs. slam routes before implementation can be validated.

scenarios.title: Bloodbowl Flat Swarm
scenarios.description: Bloodbowl reset or loose ball near centre field. Open-field convergence must produce tackles, knockdowns, and breakouts quickly — no spreading or safe carries across the flat open field.

expected_outcomes.description: Players from both teams converge quickly on the ball.
Collisions, tackles, and knockdowns occur during convergence.
A player can break from the swarm and carry in open field — but not safely all the way.

must_not_outcomes.description: Players avoid the contest zone and stay spread — no swarm forms.
Carrier runs unopposed from reset to goal with no realistic interception opportunity.

scenarios.notes: This is the canonical Bloodbowl vertical slice. The open flat geometry makes it the benchmark for swarm density and carrier danger in unobstructed space.
